title: The data black hole at the center of AI
summary: Dwarkesh Patel argues that massive training data, not sample efficiency, is the central driver of AI model capabilities. He compares human and AI learning efficiency, suggesting a fundamental asymmetry that shapes current progress trajectories.
writeup: Dwarkesh Patel makes the case that the primary driver of recent AI capabilities is the sheer scale of training data rather than algorithmic breakthroughs, using the image of a data black hole at the center of an AI galaxy. The argument centers on the sample efficiency gap: humans learn from far less data than current AI systems require, which Patel frames as the core structural problem in modern AI development. The takeaway is a reframing of where progress comes from, placing data scale ahead of algorithmic innovation and treating the human-to-AI learning efficiency disparity as the constraint that matters most.
